evals.prompt: Vectorize this logo and save as SVG: /home/viniciusrocha/projetos-pessoais/grun-marketing/landing-page/assets/image-Photoroom.png — it's a brand logo on transparent background, needs to be scalable for the website
evals.expected_output: A valid SVG file with the green logo paths, correct fill color (#00B540 or similar green), transparent background, no PIL dependency used

assertions.name: SVG file was created
assertions.check: Output SVG file exists and is not empty

assertions.name: SVG contains path elements
assertions.check: File contains at least one <path> element — means potrace actually traced something

assertions.name: Fill color is not black
assertions.check: fill attribute is NOT #000000 — means color sampling step ran and replaced the default potrace color

assertions.name: No PIL used
assertions.check: No 'from PIL' or 'import PIL' in commands run — PIL is not installed in this environment

assertions.name: Temp PBM cleaned up
assertions.check: No leftover .pbm file in output directory

evals.prompt: I need to convert this PNG to a vector file for print: /home/viniciusrocha/projetos-pessoais/grun-marketing/landing-page/assets/image-Photoroom.png — save the result in the same folder as grun-logo-v2.svg
evals.expected_output: SVG saved to the correct path, fill color sampled correctly without using PIL, at least 1 path element in the SVG

assertions.name: SVG contains path elements
assertions.check: File contains at least one <path> element

assertions.name: Fill color is green
assertions.check: Fill color hex starts with '00B' or '09C' or similar — close to the original #00B540
